subtopic: Natural Gas and Coal Sector Reforms
title: Natural Gas and Coal Sector Reforms
focus: Groups natural gas reserve estimates and pipeline tariffs with coal sector sustainability initiatives and the SHAKTI policy.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 084]
More about the news
* India’s Hydrocarbon Resource Assessment Study (HRAS) estimates hydrocarbon resources of 371 million Metric Tons of Oil Equivalent (MMTOE) in the Andaman-Nicobar (AN) Basin.
* Geologically, the AN basin lies at the intersection of the Andaman and Nicobar Basins, part of the Bengal-Arakan sedimentary system.
* Earlier, gas has been discovered in adjacent basins along North Sumatra (Indonesia) and Irrawaddy-Margui (Myanmar).
* The discovery aligns with India’s vision of establishing a Gas Based Economy by 2030 and increasing share of natural gas in its primary energy basket to 15 percent by 2030.
* Currently, India’s natural gas production meets only around 50% of its demand with rest demand being fulfilled through imports.
* Qatar, US and UAE are major sources of India’s Liquified Natural Gas (LNG) imports.
Estimated Reserves of Natural Gas in India (Total Estimated reserves: 1094.19 BCM)
* Western Offshore: 31%
* Eastern Offshore: 24%
* Assam: 15%
* Andhra Pradesh: 6%
* Rajasthan: 6%
* Gujarat: 5%
* West Bengal (CBM): 4%
* Tamil Nadu: 3%
* Tripura: 3%
* Madhya Pradesh (CBM): 2%
* Others: 1%
* Russia holds the world's largest proven natural gas reserves, followed by Iran and Qatar.
11.7.2. NATURAL GAS PIPELINE TARIFF REGULATIONS, 2025
Why in the News?
Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board (PNGRB) approves second amendment to Natural Gas Pipeline Tariff Regulations, 2025.
Key Reforms
* Streamlined Tariff Zones: It reduces the number of Unified Tariff Zones from three to two, simplifying the transportation system.
* Pipeline Development Reserve – A Sustainable Infra Model: It creates a Pipeline Development Reserve, using earnings from pipeline entities that exceed 75% utilization benchmark.
Role of PNGRB (Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board)
* Genesis: It is a statutory body formed under Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board Act, of 2006.
* Functions:
* Regulation of refining, transportation, distribution, storage, marketing, supply and sale of petroleum products and natural gas excluding production of crude oil and natural gas.
* It registers entities to market notified petroleum and petroleum products.
* Protect the interest ...

11.8. COAL SECTOR
Why in the News?
Government outlined key strategies to ensure sustainability, competitiveness of coal sector.
About Coal
- India has the world's 5th largest coal reserves, and coal accounts for 55% of the country's energy needs.
